article_title: Artemis II Crew Splashes Down Off San Diego as NASA Schedules Post-Mission Briefing and Prepares Artemis III Core Stage Rollout

content_preview.title: Artemis II Crew Splashes Down Off San Diego as NASA Schedules Post-Mission Briefing and Prepares Artemis III Core Stage Rollout
content_preview.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew returned to Earth on April 10 after the first crewed lunar voyage since 1972. A post-mission news conference is set for April 16, and the Artemis III core stage will roll out April 20.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis II mission are back on Earth, and the agency is already turning toward the next steps in its lunar program. NASA's Orion spacecraft, call sign Integrity, splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego at 8:07 p.m. EDT on April 10, 2026, closing out a nearly 10-day flight around the Moon, [according to NASA's Artemis II Flight Day 10 re-entry log](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/10/artemis-ii-flight-day-10-re-entry-live...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong space/NASA article on Artemis II mission completion and Artemis III preparations.
editor_notes.source_verification: Space.com VERIFIED via WebFetch: Orion 'Integrity' splashdown April 10 8:07 PM EDT off San Diego, crew (Wiseman, Glover, Koch, Hansen), 252,756 mi distance record, USS John P. Murtha recovery, Isaacman quote, first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 all confirmed.
editor_notes.originality: Follows April 1 Artemis II launch coverage appropriately.
